evidence: Each frame carries one load-bearing element: the aside panel in frame 3, the 0.82 readout with blank columns in frame 4, and the 'For the walk home' card in frames 5-6, with the replayed chain kept as a dimmed persistent anchor above.
No more than four novel elements appear at once; the chain is established context and each frame adds one new grouping (columns, readout, or closing prompt).
'you' and 'the posting' labels sit directly atop their column pairs and 'song'/'taste' sit beside their icons; no boxed legend or color key appears.
The 'For the walk home' card and 'Two matching lists of your own' text annotate the visible blank columns and chain rather than replacing a visual with standalone prose.
All text including the two-line walk-home question, 'score', and column headers renders well above the ~2.2% frame-height threshold and is readable.
The chain, aside panel, columns, dial, and closing card all sit fully inside the frame with margins on every edge.
No text or label overlaps another; the aside panel in frame 3 sits below the dimmed chain without collision.
Token semantics hold consistently: periwinkle input arrows, lilac score bar and 0.82 readout, and a rose crack on the dial, with no concept switching color across frames.
Every element—chain stills, columns, dial, readout, and prompt—carries propositional content; no mascots or ornaments appear.

The frames deliver the intent: the replayed chain, the 'you'/'the posting' aside multiplying into a lilac readout on the dial, blank columns for the learner, and the walk-home closing card with a chip below.
The multiply symbols sit between the two column groups and the readout sits beside the dial it feeds, keeping claims adjacent to their objects.
Across the sequence the chain replays, the aside slides in and out, and the closing card lands in reasoning order rather than materializing whole.
The compositions keep margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from chain to columns to closing prompt.
